what_it_does: Stores metadata for a channel attachment that links a provider-registry channel item to a board item (board://item-a16103e4) as a board_items attachment.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added timestamp
target uses a board:// URI scheme pointing to item-a16103e4
file body is empty after the frontmatter
standouts: attachment acts as a cross-resource pointer between a channel and a board item
concerns: No description or context explaining why this attachment exists
Target board item is opaque without resolving the URI
notes: A lightweight metadata stub that records a provider-registry channel's attachment to a specific board item.
questions: What content or relationship does board://item-a16103e4 represent?
Is this attachment bidirectional or referenced elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to serve as an attachment record; its actual content is only a metadata stub, which matches a linking purpose but lacks descriptive detail.
intention_signals: It seems intended to persist a lightweight, timestamped association between a channel entry and a board item for later retrieval or traversal.
contribution: Provides a concrete, timestamped link between a provider-registry channel attachment and a board item, giving the system a persistent association to preserve during fusion.
summary_for_embedding: A channel attachment metadata file that records a provider-registry attachment of type board_items targeting board://item-a16103e4 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To persist a dated association between a provider-registry channel record and a specific board item.
